name: English Cocker Spaniel
description: The English Cocker Spaniel is a merry, energetic gundog developed in England as an expert at flushing and retrieving woodcock in thick undergrowth, and is the most popular breed in the United Kingdom by registration numbers. More athletic and field-oriented than its American cousin, the English Cocker retains strong hunting instincts and benefits greatly from regular access to open countryside for running and retrieving. Their silky coat and long, feathered ears require weekly brushing and cleaning to prevent the ear infections and matting to which the breed is predisposed.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings

conditions_detail.name: Dilated Cardiomyopathy (DCM)
conditions_detail.description: DCM is a disease of the heart muscle causing the chambers to enlarge and the walls to thin, reducing the heart's pumping efficiency. It progresses to congestive heart failure if untreated and is a leading cause of cardiac death in large breeds.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Management with diuretics, ACE inhibitors, Pimobendan, and antiarrhythmics can prolong life significantly. No cure exists; treatment is palliative.
prevention_tips: Annual cardiac screening for at-risk breeds
Avoid grain-free diets in predisposed breeds (FDA advisory)
Taurine supplementation investigated in some breeds
